content.text: The AI bubble has finally popped.
Anthropic just lost $200 billion dollars
off of their private market valuation in
just a few hours. XAI and OpenAI are
seeing the same thing. All of the
naysayers, all of the people who have
been calling and predicting the AI
bubble to pop, it turns out they were
actually right.
Oh, actually, no. It's Anthropics
lawyers who just put out a notice to
everybody who's been buying these shady
secondary shares that they are now
voided. All right, so there's so much to
talk about, including my own personal
experience trying to buy secondary
anthropic shares, and I want to talk
about all of it. And by the way, if you
like me breaking down stories just like
this, hit the like button, subscribe to
the channel. It really does help spread
the word. Thank you in advance. So,
Anthropic is one of, if not the fastest
growing private company of all time.
soon potentially to be the biggest
monopoly of all time, but I'll save that
for another video. Look at this growth
simply from February of this year. They
went from a $300 billion valuation all
the way up to above 1 trillion. And
here's the thing, back when it was about
300 billion, I actually had the
opportunity to purchase these secondary
shares that I just told you about, but I
didn't. And I was really regretful for a
while until today. So this is the letter
that the lawyers just published.
Unauthorized enthropic stock sales and
investment scams. We are sharing
important information about investments
and anthropic to help protect
individuals from potentially invalid
transfers or investment fraud. And while
I read this, I want you to keep in mind
that you as an individual, me as an
individual, do not get to participate
like big investment companies in one of
the biggest value creation events in the
history of humanity with the AI boom.
And I'm going to talk more about that
later because it seems so backwards and
unfair. So transfer restrictions. Both
our preferred stock and common stock are
subject to transfer restrictions
contained in our bylaws. Any sale or
transfer of anthropic stock or any
interest in anthropic stock that has not
been approved by our board of directors
is void and will not be recognized on
our books and records. So what is going
on? Anthropic is a private company and
that is the opposite of a public company
like Nvidia or Apple or Microsoft and
Google where you can go to whatever
brokerage firm you use and buy shares of
those public companies. And because
Anthropic is a private company, it is
very difficult for individual investors
to purchase shares in the company. But
occasionally there are certain events in
which early employees want to sell some
of their shares while the company's
still public. And during those events,
that's when individuals or large venture
capitalist firms are able to buy up some
of those shares. And with all of the
hype around artificial intelligence and
specifically the growth rate of
Anthropics revenue over the last 6
months, there has been such demand to
try to get some of these Anthropic
shares. And that is what caused their
private market valuation to absolutely
skyrocket over the last few months.
Daario just said that their revenue had
grown 80 times this year alone. 80x.
That is absolutely unheard of. And so
what we've seen is this black market of
picking up anthropic shares. Basically,
an employee will want to sell some of
their shares, somebody else will get
them, and then attempt to resell them
because the demand is skyrocketing. So
there's an arbitrage opportunity there.
But because there's so much money,
because there's such a big arbitrage
opportunity, more and more middlemen
have gotten in the game. So, we have
anthropic shares on one side and then
multiple layers of people just charging
fees to get those shares. And it's not
only extremely expensive to try to
purchase some of these shares, but it's
also completely opaque. You won't
actually know what you're purchasing
because there's so many layers between
you and the actual shares. And by the
way, you know what's also incredibly
valuable? The sponsor of today's video.
I use GPile every single day. It reviews
all of the code I commit using AI and it
has caught some serious bugs before I
deployed it to production. But most
people don't realize that Grapile
actually gets more personalized over
time. It learns how you like to code,
your best practices, the syntax you like
to use and will actually store these as
memories and get better and give you
better recommendations as it learns
about you and your coding team. And I
want to actually talk about the three
learning modes that it has. first giving
it complete access to your codebase. It
indexes your codebase when you sign up.
This leads to highquality reviews right
out of the box. And it's not just
reviewing the PR that you're looking at.
It kind of sees around corners. For
example, it can detect a change in one
file that might break another file three
steps away from it. And then last
signals from PRs. Grapile acts like
another member of your team, not some
completely automated isolated tool. And
the combination of these three tools
mimics how a human engineer would
operate on your team. It gets better
with experience. So check out Graptile.
I use them. They are a great partner to
the channel. I'm going to drop a 14-day
free trial link down in the description.
Go check them out. Let them know I sent
you. And now back to the video. So let
me show you what this actually looks
like. Over here you have a very happy
anthropic employee who has a ton of
shares of anthropic stock. They are now
multi-millionaires and they are looking
to derisk. They are looking to sell some
of their shares because they want to go
buy a house. And so they sell their
shares to a buyer. The buyer is actually
able to buy from a bunch of different
employees.
And then what that person does or that
company is they wrap it all up and then
they put it all into something called an
SPV, a specialurpose vehicle. Now this
SPV houses all of these shares and what
do they do? Well, they try to resell it.
They allow investment into the special
purpose vehicle. So you have all these
other people on the outside trying to
purchase
shares of this SPV, but that's not free.
So, of course, they want to make money
beyond just selling the shares and the
increase in value, they want to make
fees. And the normal fee to invest in a
vehicle like this is usually 2%. But
because shares are in such high demand,
these SPVS charge a lot more. They are
charging upwards of 10% if not more. And
then we have a bunch of people buying
SPVS. And then what does this
enterprising person do? Well, they
think, "Hey, why can't I broker more of
these shares out?" And they do that. So,
you have this original SPV, which is,
let's say, 2% fees, and then you have a
wrapper around it for another SPV,
which, let's say that's 5% fees, and
then potentially another one, and then
all of a sudden, you're seeing 10% fees.
And so by the time the end investor gets
it, they are paying so much in fees and
they have very little visibility into
what all the terms are along the way. So
we have this investor on the outside who
actually I should probably make them sad
and they're buying this SPV through that
SPV through this one all the way trying
to get those shares, but they're just
paying immense fees and almost zero
visibility. And it turns out it was
actually incredibly risky because often
times they were never allowed to
purchase the shares to begin with. So
now back to the post by Anthropics
lawyers. Here they're basically saying
be warned, be careful. If we have not
directly approved the sale of shares
from one of our employees, they are
void. And that's the problem. It is
almost impossible to see through the
layers of SPVS to actually purchase the
shares. So let's continue. This means
that if someone purports to sell
Enthropic shares without proper board
approval, that transaction is invalid.
The purported buyer would not be
recognized as a stockholder of Enthropic
and you would have no stockholder
rights. So layers of SPVS, null and
void. And that doesn't mean all
secondary sales are invalid or illegal
or shady. Some of them are legitimate,
but then some of those get wrapped up in
illegitimate SPVS and then the entire
thing becomes void. And look what they
say right here. We do not permit
specialurpose vehicles to acquire
anthropic stock and any transfer of
shares to an SPV are void under our
transfer restrictions
right there. This is why we're seeing
such a massive decline in the valuation
of Enthropic on the private market. Not
because the demand isn't there, but
because they've basically cut off
demand. because that demand has no valid
way to get the shares. I definitely
would like to, but it's almost
impossible to do so legitimately. Offers
to invest in Anthropic's past or future
financing rounds through an SVV are
prohibited. And they continue, "We are
also aware of investment funds that
claim to offer indirect access to
anthropic stock. These funds often
market themselves by saying something
like, you can't invest in Enthropic
directly, but invest with us and we'll
get access for you." And in fact, they
specifically called out a bunch of firms
that do exactly this. I'll get to that
in a moment. These funds are most likely
relying on mechanisms that attempt to
circumvent our transfer restrictions.
Any third party claiming to sell
anthropic shares to the general public,
whether through direct sales, forward
contracts, tokenized securities, or
other mechanisms is likely either
engaged in fraud or offering an
investment that may have no value due to
our transfer restrictions. So basically,
if you bought some of these, you're kind
of out of luck. Okay, so they
specifically said, "Here's what to watch
out for. If someone contacts you
unsolicited via email, social media, or
messaging apps offering anthropic
shares, claims to have exclusive or
limited time access to enthropic stock,
requests payment via cryptocurrency,
wire transfer, or other hardto-race
methods, pressures you to invest
quickly. I've never seen this with
enthropic shares. We have people dying
to buy these entropic shares. So them
saying people are using these high
pressure techniques seems surprising to
me. Claims to have structured a
transaction to avoid company
restrictions cannot provide
documentation to board approval for the
transfer. This last one is the one I'd
be most worried about. The SPVS that
I've come across purporting to have
anthropic shares to invest in basically
give zero visibility into the actual
board approval. And specifically here
are the unauthorized firms. Open Door
Partners, Unicorns Exchange,
Pachchamama, I've never even heard of
that, Lionhe Heart Ventures, Hive,
Forge, Sidecar, and Up Market. And some
of these companies are actually legit.
They sell secondary shares. That is what
they do. There needs to be a liquid
market for private companies, and that's
what some of these companies do. But
again, unless you have explicit
authorization by the board of directors
of Anthropic, you cannot do that. You
cannot buy it. And if you buy it,
they're void. You're wasting your money.
You're basically just losing your money.
And it has gotten so chaotic that there
are even people posting publicly on
Twitter about selling shares that first
of all, they shouldn't be selling, but
also because they're not a registered
broker is completely illegal. And that's
what we're seeing here with Ashurora.
She posted this and then quickly deleted
it. Simply brokering an anthropic
secondary deal made me more money than
my entire net worth from working in my
20s. This is insane. Now, of course, I
saw this and I contacted her. I DM' her.
I said, "Hey, I'd like some shares." And
it didn't go anywhere, but the point is
she deleted it because she's not a
registered broker. And people were
saying, "Hey, people are going to
contact FINRA. This is completely
illegal. You cannot do this. You will go
to jail if you do this." And one funny
thing I just want to point out, she's a
VC and she got Forbes 30 under 30, but
she said she's not arrested. And if
you're not familiar with Forbes 30
under30, there is this ongoing joke that
people on the Forbes 30 under 30 keep
going to jail. And here's a few
examples. Sam Bankman Freed, Caroline
Ellison, Charlie Javis, Martin Skrey,
Joanna Smith Griffin, and the list goes
on and on. So Ash herself was making fun
of the joke because she's on the Forbes
30 under 30 and is not arrested, but she
is definitely tempting fate here. And so
the real problem is that average people
don't have access to anthropic shares,
open AAI shares, XAI shares. These are
generational companies that will have
some of the biggest value creation in
human history. and only the top tier
venture capitalists and uber wealthy are
able to acquire shares and participate
in this private market. Now I understand
there are reasons why private
unsophisticated investors have a higher
chance of getting screwed. They have a
higher chance of falling for fraud than
sophisticated accredited investors. But
at the same time, this is very much the
rich get richer. And it would be so much
better if we just loosened the rules and
allowed more people to participate in
these private market sales. Here's an
example of what happens when a private
company sells shares. It was just
reported today that Anthropic let 600
employees sell a total of $6.6 billion
in shares for an average of 1 million
per employee. That is great. They want
some liquidity for their years of hard
work. And then really the only people
who can buy up these shares are people
who are already on the inside. They are
venture capital firms or very wealthy
individuals. And so then what happens?
Well, we have a whole swath of new
millionaires, multi-millionaires on the
market, driving up the price of San
Francisco real estate, making it even
harder for the average person to have
residence here. Just in the last couple
months, this is what we've seen. San
Francisco home sale in the Sunset
District at $900,000 over list price.
That is nearly 90% more than the list
price. And this is not the only example
of this. I've seen a dozen examples of
houses in the last few months in the San
Francisco Bay area go for sale so
absurdly above the list price. This is
because there's just so much new money
slloshing around. And again, only a few
people are really able to capture the
value. And all of these secondary sales
are just a reaction to the fact that
private companies are staying private
longer. Anthropic is valued at a
trillion dollars. Open AAI similarly,
XAI, same thing. Even Stripe, a nonAI
company, has been private way longer
than anybody would have anticipated. And
that means the employees cannot sell the
shares. That also means that average
people can't just go to the public
market and purchase those shares. And so
where does that leave us? Well, that
leaves us with insanely valuable private
companies that the average retail
investor cannot get their hands on,
cannot participate in. one of the
biggest value creations, one of the
biggest generational wealth creations in
human history. And of course, so many
people are pointing at artificial
intelligence as a vehicle to increase
the disparity of wealth in the United
States and worldwide. And it just
furthers that case. So, you're either on
the inside and you are one of the
biggest venture capital firms in the
world and then you have access to it or
you're one of the wealthiest individuals
in the world and you're on the inside
and you can get these deals because
you're just friends with everybody or
you're on the outside looking in. And by
the way, I really do wish I had
anthropic shares right now after the
story from last week. Details right
here.
